value_chain_position: Precision fuel and combustion control systems for aerospace engines (commercial + military) and industrial gas turbines (power generation); sells engineered sub-systems embedded in turbines as a sole-source or dual-source Tier 1 supplier.
relative_comps: Woodward supplies fuel management systems for CFM LEAP, GE9X, and multiple military engines — deeply embedded, long-qualification-cycle components that are costly to dual-source. Revenue CAGR of 14% and FCF margin of ~10% are solid but not exceptional. The energy_transition angle is genuine: Woodward's industrial segment supplies fuel control and actuation for gas turbines used in grid peaking and distributed generation, and hydrogen-compatible fuel system R&D is underway. At $22B market cap, Woodward trades at a modest premium to Moog (which has weaker economics) but a meaningful discount to TransDigm and Heico.
